text.title: EU AI Act Compliance — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `eu-ai-act-compliance` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Classify every AI system risk tier BEFORE any architectural decision; retrofitting compliance costs more than designing for it.
rule.rationale: Risk tier determines which Articles apply, documentation depth, and conformity-assessment path.

rule.statement: Do not deploy any system matching an Article 5 prohibited practice regardless of claimed benefit.
rule.rationale: Article 5 is absolute — no proportionality test. Fines EUR 35M or 7% global turnover. Effective Feb 2025.

rule.statement: If the system maps to Annex III, treat as high-risk and apply Articles 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 17, 43.
rule.rationale: Annex III enumerates 8 domains where AI decisions affect fundamental rights. High-risk obligations apply Aug 2026.

rule.statement: For chatbots, synthetic-content generators, emotion recognition, biometric categorisation: disclose AI involvement clearly and at the earliest opportunity.
rule.rationale: Article 50 requires transparency where users may assume human interaction or be misled.

rule.statement: GPAI providers MUST publish a model card + training-data summary; >10^25 FLOPs models MUST also red-team and report serious incidents.
rule.rationale: Systemic-risk threshold targets foundation models with broad societal impact. Enforcement active Aug 2026.

rule.statement: Every high-risk system MUST provide effective human-oversight controls (override, stop, audit log) per Article 14.
rule.rationale: Automated rejection with no override path violates Article 14 — design constraint, not a UI add-on.

rule.statement: Every requirement in a compliance draft MUST cite the specific Article / Annex number.

rationale.source: EU AI Act drafting practice; Kennedys Law 2026

rule.rationale: Articles are the contract. Vague compliance prose is unauditable.
